description_long: This sculpture depicts a stylized, partially ruined stone archway set atop a circular wooden base that resembles sand or earth. The arch is constructed from large, blocky gray stones, with two upright columns supporting a curved keystone structure. Scattered around the base are several broken column fragments, suggesting the remnants of a classical architectural ruin. Thin metal rods or stakes are embedded in the base, possibly representing survey markers or supports. The overall composition evokes the feeling of ancient ruins, blending classical architectural elements with a diorama-like presentation. The materials and textures give the impression of weathered stone and polished wood.
description: A miniature ruined stone archway with broken columns on a circular wooden base, evoking ancient architectural ruins.
description_view_0: The front view shows the intact archway with two upright columns and several broken column pieces scattered on the wooden base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the arch's depth and the arrangement of broken columns, with the arch appearing tilted backward.
description_view_2: From this angle, the archway is prominent, and the broken columns are more visible on the right side of the base.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the arch's thickness and the distribution of column fragments, with the arch leaning to the left.
attribute_subject_matter: ruined classical stone archway and columns
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is presented on a display base, suggesting an indoor setting such as a gallery, museum, or exhibition space.
